Home.title: Nerina Berthelot

About.title: About me
About.description: How I got into systems design
About.paragraph1: I came to systems design through a different path. I started in Fine Arts and Public Art, working in institutions and communities where context always shapes what gets built.
About.paragraph2: That experience trained one core habit: understanding relationships, roles and conditions before proposing structure. Nothing exists in isolation; products evolve inside networks.
About.paragraph3: When I moved into technology through QA and then UX/UI, the same questions returned. What gives digital structure meaning, and what allows it to be adopted in real environments?
About.paragraph4: Today I design systems, not just interfaces. I prioritize internal logic, flow quality and sustainable decision-making so products respond clearly and consistently to user needs.
